description_long: This house features a simple rectangular structure with a steeply pitched gable roof clad in reddish-orange tiles. The exterior walls are constructed from gray brickwork, giving it a sturdy and traditional appearance. One long side of the house is dominated by a large, horizontal window that spans nearly the entire width, providing ample natural light and a modern touch. The opposite long side is windowless, emphasizing privacy. Both gable ends are solid brick with no visible doors or windows, suggesting the main entryway may be on the windowed side or not visible in these views. The overall design is minimalistic, blending classic materials with a contemporary window feature.
description: A rectangular brick house with a steep red gable roof and a large horizontal window on one side, combining traditional and modern elements.
description_view_0: End view showing a solid gray brick gable wall and the red-tiled roof peak.
description_view_1: Side view featuring a large horizontal window set in a gray brick wall beneath the red-tiled roof.
description_view_2: Opposite end view, identical to view 0, with a solid gray brick gable wall and no openings.
description_view_3: Side view with a continuous gray brick wall and no windows or doors, under the red-tiled roof.
attribute_entryway_design: Entryway not visible; likely integrated into the windowed side, possibly with a minimalist door design.
